revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Totenfest des Sonnenauges]]
'''Festival of the Sun's Eye''' is an ancient Egyptian festival ==Overview== [[Image:Eye of Ra 3.svg|thumb|The Eye of Ra]] The '''Festival of the Death of the Solar Eye''' (also '''Festival of the Death of the {{ill|Eye of Ra|en|Eye of Ra|qid=Q760218|lt=Eye of Ra|1=Eye of Ra}}''') was celebrated annually in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} on the {{ill|17th of Peret I|de|17. Peret I|ja|第1ペレト月17日|zh|第一季涌月17日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/17th of Peret I|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its origins date back to the {{ill|Prehistoric Egypt|en|Prehistoric Egypt|qid=Q208520|lt=Predynastic Egypt|1=Prehistoric Egypt}}. Evidence is available from the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} onwards. The 17th of Peret I originally corresponded to around October 18, marking the end of the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|qid=Q1519552|lt=Nile flood|1=Flooding of the Nile}} in the {{ill|Nile Delta|en|Nile Delta|qid=Q179528|lt=Nile Delta|1=Nile Delta}}. Seventeen days prior, on the {{ill|30th of Achet IV|de|30. Achet IV|ja|第4アケト月30日|zh|第四季洪水月30日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/30th of Achet IV|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the rebirth of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}} was celebrated, symbolizing the emergence of flooded lands. == Background == === Egyptian Mythology === In {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|qid=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}}, bearers of the Eye of Ra are also known as daughters of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}}. Prominent daughters of Ra include {{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|qid=Q133343|lt=Hathor|1=Hathor}}, {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|qid=Q930167|lt=Sopdet|1=Sopdet}}, {{ill|Sekhmet|en|Sekhmet|qid=Q146104|lt=Sekhmet|1=Sekhmet}}, {{ill|Bastet|en|Bastet|qid=Q129106|lt=Bastet|1=Bastet}}, {{ill|Tefnut|en|Tefnut|qid=Q116273|lt=Tefnut|1=Tefnut}}, and {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|qid=Q79876|lt=Isis|1=Isis}}. In the myth "{{ill|Book of the Heavenly Cow|de|Buch von der Himmelskuh|ja|天の雌牛の書|zh|天牛之书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Heavenly Cow|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", the goddess of the Solar Eye holds a significant role, similarly featured in the "{{ill|Myth of the Solar Eye|de|Mythos vom Sonnenauge|ja|太陽の目の神話|zh|太阳之眼神话|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myth of the Solar Eye|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}", which describes the death of the Solar Eye: {{Quote|He (Ra) soon lovingly named his daughter "his {{ill|Diadem|en|Diadem|qid=Q746591|lt=diadem|1=Diadem}}" and also "his Eye". When she died, she asked her father: "Set up my image once every year so that I may look up to you."<ref>Adolf Erman: ''Die Religion der Ägypter.'' Berlin et al., 2001, p. 66.</ref>}} In later periods, Hathor, as bearer of the Eye of Ra, was particularly revered. On the 17th of Peret I, priests carried her golden statue onto temple roofs throughout Egypt so she could look up to her father. Archaeologists have discovered numerous rooftop chapels similar to the chapel of the {{ill|Festival of Hathor|de|Hathor-Fest|ja|ハトホル祭|zh|哈索尔节|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival of Hathor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Dendera|en|Dendera|qid=Q29019|lt=Dendera|1=Dendera}}. However, the exact procedure of the festival remains uncertain. === Historical Context === With the final retreat of the Nile to normal levels and the start of {{ill|Sowing|en|Sowing|qid=Q777671|lt=sowing|1=Sowing}}, the cycle of festivals concluded: New Year’s Festival, death of Osiris, arrival of the Solar Eye, search for Osiris, rebirth of Osiris, conception of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}, and death of the Solar Eye. == Literature == * {{ill|Adolf Erman|en|Adolf Erman|qid=Q61421|lt=Adolf Erman|1=Adolf Erman}}: ''Die Religion der Ägypter. Ihr Werden und Vergehen in vier Jahrtausenden.'' 2nd ed., de Gruyter, Berlin et al. 2001, ISBN 3-11-017040-X. * Carola Metzner-Nebelsick (ed.): ''Rituale in der Vorgeschichte, Antike und Gegenwart. Studien zur Vorderasiatischen, Prähistorischen und Klassischen Archäologie, Ägyptologie, Alten Geschichte, Theologie und Religionswissenschaft. Interdisziplinäre Tagung vom 1.–2. Februar 2002 an der Freien Universität Berlin.'' Leidorf, Rahden 2003, ISBN 3-89646-434-5. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|qid=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization, vol. 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}}). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|zh|西格弗里德·肖特|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse, vol. 10, 1950, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al. 1950. == External links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20111121000244/http://www.alice-dsl.net/a.effland/Waitkus_Dachkapellen_Edfu_Begleitheft_5.pdf Rooftop chapels for funeral rites] (PDF; 2.70 MB) == References == <references/>
==References== {{translated page|de|Totenfest des Sonnenauges|version=234674601|comment=Imported full de history}}
[[ja:太陽の目の死者祭]] [[de:Totenfest des Sonnenauges]]
